2603.18863.title: Why Better Cross-Lingual Alignment Fails for Better Cross-Lingual Transfer: Case of Encoders
2603.18863.paper_summary: Better cross-lingual alignment is often assumed to yield better cross-lingual transfer. However, explicit alignment techniques -- despite increasing embedding similarity -- frequently fail to improve token-level downstream performance. In this work, we show that this mismatch arises because alignment and downstream task objectives are largely orthogonal, and because the downstream benefits from alignment vary substantially across languages and task types. We analyze four XLM-R encoder models aligned on different language pairs and fine-tuned for either POS Tagging or Sentence Classification. Using representational analyses, including embedding distances, gradient similarities, and gradient magnitudes for both task and alignment losses, we find that: (1) embedding distances alone are unreliable predictors of improvements (or degradations) in task performance and (2) alignment and task gradients are often close to orthogonal, indicating that optimizing one objective may contribute little to optimizing the other. Taken together, our findings explain why ``better'' alignment often fails to translate into ``better'' cross-lingual transfer. Based on these insights, we provide practical guidelines for combining cross-lingual alignment with task-specific fine-tuning, highlighting the importance of careful loss selection.
2603.18863.paper_summary_zh: 更好的跨語言對齊通常被認為能夠帶來更好的跨語言轉移。<lb/>然而，儘管顯式對齊技術提高了嵌入相似性，但它們在提升標記級下游性能方面經常失敗。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們顯示這種不匹配的原因在於對齊和下游任務目標在很大程度上是正交的，並且來自對齊的下游好處在不同語言和任務類型之間變化很大。<lb/>我們分析了四個在不同語言對上對齊並針對詞性標註或句子分類進行微調的XLM-R編碼器模型。<lb/>通過表示分析，包括嵌入距離、梯度相似性和任務及對齊損失的梯度大小，我們發現：（1）僅僅依賴嵌入距離並不是任務性能改善（或惡化）的可靠預測指標，並且（2）對齊和任務梯度通常接近正交，這表明優化一個目標可能對優化另一個目標貢獻不大。<lb/>綜合來看，我們的發現解釋了為什麼「更好的」對齊往往無法轉化為「更好的」跨語言轉移。<lb/>基於這些見解，我們提供了將跨語言對齊與任務特定微調相結合的實用指導，強調了仔細選擇損失的重要性。
2603.18863.author: Yana Veitsman et.al.
2603.18863.authors: Yana Veitsman, Yihong Liu, Hinrich Schütze
